message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@mayank.dhillon-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
*Incident Number INC100052:* P1 payment failure and intermittent website open failure
I'm seeing a significant spike in failed payment transactions—about 40% of customer checkouts are not completing successfully right now.
What regions are affected? I need exact numbers on impacted customers and orders. Are we currently losing revenue?
@daniel @shay is this related to the change that @tinus was mentioning?
:star: Reviewed changes
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
Have we made any change in the system
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
Yes, my PaymentService change (CHG89362) was deployed 5 minutes ago, but the new Bitcoin payment code isn't being called yet and has solid test coverage.
I deployed a CheckoutService change (CHG89360) to production about 1 hour ago, which mainly refactored the payment initiation logic but it's safe to rollback if needed.
There was intermittent spike of 500 at around 17:30, @tanya can we check the cause
:star: Manage actions and timings
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
@shay if it is just being deployed, and are safe to revert, let's revert them if there is no harm. @daniel to verify
I will rollback my checkout change right away
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
@bob we are facing payment failures, can we ask the agents to handle the customers that team is working on quick fix for this.
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
I need specifics: how many customers are unable to pay right now? Which regions are impacted? Is there an estimate for when this will be resolved?
@tanya can we review other metrics, are all other service metrics stable?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
All other service metrics look stable except for the payment flow, which is still showing elevated errors in the UK region.
@shay anything else that we might've changed
No, my checkout service rollback was the only recent change from my side and nothing else has been deployed by me.
@tanya is this region specific. Are all other regions stable
Yes, the elevated payment failures are isolated to the UK region, and all other regions are stable at this time.
@shay @daniel are we aware of any change that might impact UK?
I don't have any changes that would specifically affect the UK, and my rollback didn't resolve the issue.
My change shouldn't impact the UK specifically, as the Bitcoin payment code isn't active for that region and all my tests passed.
@tanya are there any resource which are this region specific that might've been impacted
The only region-specific resources involved are the payment processing libraries and endpoints for GBP transactions, which are unique to the UK flow.
any unusual pattern?
The only unusual pattern is that payment failures remain at 100% in the UK, while all other regions are unaffected.
@channel seeing error for GW error due to high load, are we aware of it
:star: Qualify the problem
I'm not seeing any signs of resource exhaustion or high load on the gateway or backend services in our metrics.
Customer complaints from the UK are still coming in at the same rate, with no improvement after the last rollback.
The gateway timeout errors are only appearing for UK payment flows, and overall system load remains normal.
is there any abnormal traffic in uk region? from any specific IP or any script running at our end?
I've checked the traffic patterns and there is no abnormal spike or suspicious activity from any specific IP or internal script in the UK region.
Any specific payment method that is affected for uk or all
All payment methods for UK customers are failing, not just a specific one.
@tanya can we deep dive on what is causing the load
I've started a deep dive into the UK payment flow and will focus on recent changes and shared components that could be causing the increased load.
@hamed can you also check
Online now but I've been following the conversation. I can take a look and let you know what I find
I can see from the user experience that it's failing at the payments stage, and I can also see HTTP 504 in payments
@bob it seems only UK is impacted
This is a clear indication that the checkout service is working, but not the payments
Team is checking. All payment methods are failing atm.
I strongly suggest we look into payment service right away
Understood, UK only. How many customers or orders are affected so far? Are we seeing a measurable drop in revenue at this moment? I need numbers.
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
@shay can we please check
I've double-checked my changes and the rollback is confirmed, so the issue isn't coming from my side.
@daniel can you please help in checking for payment service related metrics and changes
I can check the payment service metrics, but my recent change was only to the Bitcoin payment code and all my unit tests passed.
@daniel can we revert those or kill those changes
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
@daniel please ensure that this shouldn't break anything existing
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
@hamed can you please help in verifying the metrics
I've double-checked the rollback and it restores the previous stable version, so there shouldn't be any impact on existing functionality.
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I'm on it and will review the payment metrics to confirm if the rollback resolved the issue.
@tanya can you please check the cart flow
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
All payment methods down in the UK—confirm the number of impacted customers and current revenue loss. I need an ETA for resolution.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Seems to be working now for me
:star: Validate the fix
Great to hear! For full confirmation, could we ask Bob from Customer Service to check if customer transactions are back to normal on his end?
Awesome! Let's have Bob confirm that customer metrics are healthy before we close this out.
@bob seems to have resolved now. Currently seems to be related to Bitcoing related changes which team has reverted. Team will do an internal RCA on this.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
@maya can you please also review this
@channel let's cross verify all the metrics for all the services once
I've rechecked the cart flow and everything looks normal from my side. For a complete picture, let's confirm with Bob if customer-facing metrics are back to baseline.
Security systems remain quiet—no alerts or anomalies detected. Suggest we check with Bob for the latest customer status to ensure everything is resolved externally.
@bob can you please help here
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
If resolved, confirm that payments are processing and revenue is back to normal. I want verification that orders are going through now.
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
